summary: This is an extremely brief post—just a single sentence—where Joel acknowledges that a CityDesk bug fix release broke existing functionality for users. He links to a discussion forum thread about the issue. The post serves as a public acknowledgment of the problem and an implicit apology to affected users.
key_points: Bug fix releases that introduce new problems are worse than the original bugs
Joel publicly acknowledged CityDesk's regression rather than staying silent
The post directs users to the community forum for discussion of the issue
money_quotes: There's nothing worse than upgrading to a supposed "bug fix" release that makes your stuff stop working
key_insight: A bug fix release that breaks existing functionality is worse than the original bug it was meant to fix.
